21.reason: The loop condition should be c[i-1] + 1 < c[i+1] to match the other cases and avoid printing the end character. Currently it prints one extra character (F) because the loop runs while c[i-1] < c[i+1], but the character printed is c[i-1]+1, so when c[i-1] becomes equal to c[i+1]-1, it prints c[i+1] and then increments, causing the end character to be printed.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Same issue: modifying c[i-1] corrupts the array, and the loop condition is incorrect (should be c[i-1]+1 < c[i+1]).

Currently it prints one extra character (F) because the loop runs while c[i-1] < c[i+1], but the character printed is c[i-1]+1, so when c[i-1] becomes equal to c[i+1]-1, it prints c[i+1] and then increments, causing the end character to be printed.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 1.0 , reason:  The loop condition should be `c[i - 1] + 1 < c[i + 1]`, not `c[i - 1] < c[i + 1]`, because it prints characters from c[i-1]+1 up to but not including c[i+1]. Using `<` causes an extra character (the ending character) to be printed.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 1.0 , reason:  Loop condition should be c[i - 1] + 1 < c[i + 1] to avoid printing the right boundary character again later.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 1.0 , reason:  The loop condition c[i - 1] < c[i + 1] causes the loop to execute one extra iteration, printing the end character (c[i+1]) again. It should be c[i - 1] + 1 < c[i + 1] to match the logic of the other two cases and prevent duplicate output.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 1.0 , reason:  The loop condition incorrectly uses c[i - 1] < c[i + 1] instead of c[i - 1] + 1 < c[i + 1]. This causes the loop to print the end character (e.g., 'F'), which is then printed again by the main loop when i reaches that index, resulting in a duplicate character in the output.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 1.0 , reason:  The loop condition should be c[i - 1] + 1 < c[i + 1] to match the pattern of other cases and avoid printing one extra character. This causes the last character of the range to be printed twice when combined with the next non-'-' character.
